title: Improvisations: Light And Snow: 14
content:
Like an old tree uprooted by the wind
    And flung down cruelly
    With roots bared to the sun and stars
    And limp leaves brought to earth
    Torn from its house
    So do I seem to myself
    When you have left me.
meaning: About: Conrad Potter Aiken (August 5, 1889 � August 17, 1973) was a Pulitzer Prize-winning American author, born in Savannah, Georgia, whose work includes poetry, short stories, novels, and an autobiography.
